sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.46 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Can the question 7.28 on conditions for admissibility of an elementary carpet${ A} = \{ { A}_r r \}$be reduced to Lie rank 1 if$K$is a field? A carpet${ A}$of type$$of additive subgroups of$K$is called addmissible\/ if in the Chevalley group over$K$associated with the root system$$the subgroup$< x_r({ A}_r) r >$intersects$x_r(K)$in$x_r({ A}_r)$. More precisely, is it true that the carpet${ A}$is a...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
